one_time_description: Note receivable from Frog Lake Energy Resources Limited Partnership of $6,000,000 was forgiven during the current fiscal year
economic_notes: Nation has significant oil and gas interests through Frog Lake Energy Resources Corp and related entities. Also operates oilfield services, off-reserve housing, travel centre, ranching, midstream, and construction enterprises. Investment in Government Business Partnerships includes Seven Lakes Oilfield Services LP, Pimee Well Services LP, Pimee Well Services Ltd, IMI Brokerage LP. COVID-19 resulted in temporary employee terminations and remote work arrangements.
notes: 14 entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs) including oil and gas, housing, construction, and other enterprises. Note receivable of $6M from Frog Lake Energy Resources LP was forgiven in the year - significant one-time item. Morning Sky Health and Wellness Society revenues flow through the Nation. Federal Trust Funds of $4,251,936 held by ISC on behalf of the Nation. CMHC replacement reserve of $234,645 is unfunded. COVID-19 note included with uncertainty disclosures. Change in purpose of $2,347,768 approved to cover prior year Community Programs deficits. ISC funding in revenue lines includes $140,323 clawback recovery; consolidated ISC total per segmented note is $29,643,642 but income statement shows $29,503,319. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $29,503,319 + HRDC $752,682 + CMHC $193,524 + ISC clawback recovery $140,323 = $30,589,848; osr = $43,635,043 - $30,589,848 = $13,045,195. Restricted cash includes Province of Alberta $149,133, Legal and Housing $9,177, Capital projects $3,000,360.
